org.openaq.Locations.Kafka.description: Kafka endpoint for OpenAQ Location reference CloudEvents on topic `openaq`, keyed by stable `{location_id}`.

org.openaq.Sensors.Kafka.description: Kafka endpoint for OpenAQ Sensor reference and Measurement telemetry CloudEvents on topic `openaq`, keyed by stable `{location_id}/{sensor_id}`.

org.openaq.Mqtt.description: MQTT 5 producer endpoint for OpenAQ CloudEvents using an air-quality Unified Namespace topic hierarchy.

org.openaq.Amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 producer endpoint for OpenAQ CloudEvents on the configured broker address.

org.openaq.locations.description: Transport-neutral OpenAQ monitoring-location reference events keyed by the stable OpenAQ location id.

org.openaq.Location.name: Location
org.openaq.Location.description: Reference catalog metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before measurement events and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ.

org.openaq.sensors.description: Transport-neutral OpenAQ sensor reference and latest-measurement telemetry events keyed by the stable location/sensor id tuple.

org.openaq.Sensor.name: Sensor
org.openaq.Sensor.description: Reference catalog metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and most recent value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it.

org.openaq.Measurement.name: Measurement
org.openaq.Measurement.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor.

org.openaq.locations.kafka.description: Kafka bindings for OpenAQ location reference events.

org.openaq.kafka.Location.name: Location
org.openaq.kafka.Location.description: Reference catalog metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before measurement events and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ.

org.openaq.sensors.kafka.description: Kafka bindings for OpenAQ sensor reference and latest-measurement events.

org.openaq.kafka.Sensor.name: Sensor
org.openaq.kafka.Sensor.description: Reference catalog metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and most recent value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it.

org.openaq.kafka.Measurement.name: Measurement
org.openaq.kafka.Measurement.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor.

org.openaq.locations.mqtt.description: MQTT 5 retained reference topic for OpenAQ locations.

org.openaq.mqtt.Location.name: Location
org.openaq.mqtt.Location.description: Reference catalog metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before measurement events and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ.

org.openaq.sensors.mqtt.description: MQTT 5 retained reference and latest-value topics for OpenAQ sensors.

org.openaq.mqtt.Sensor.name: Sensor
org.openaq.mqtt.Sensor.description: Reference catalog metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and most recent value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it.

org.openaq.mqtt.Measurement.name: Measurement
org.openaq.mqtt.Measurement.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor.

org.openaq.locations.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 bindings for OpenAQ location reference events.

org.openaq.amqp.Location.name: Location

subject.description: AMQP message subject matching the CloudEvent subject and Kafka key for the OpenAQ location.

country_iso.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code used for broker filters and MQTT hierarchy.

x-opt-partition-key.description: Service Bus/Event Hubs partition key matching CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

org.openaq.amqp.Location.description: Reference catalog metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before measurement events and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ.

org.openaq.sensors.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 bindings for OpenAQ sensor reference and measurement events.

org.openaq.amqp.Sensor.name: Sensor

subject.description: AMQP message subject matching the CloudEvent subject and Kafka key for the OpenAQ sensor.

parameter_name.description: OpenAQ parameter name routing attribute such as pm25, pm10, o3, no2, so2, co, or bc.

x-opt-partition-key.description: Service Bus/Event Hubs partition key matching CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

org.openaq.amqp.Sensor.description: Reference catalog metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and most recent value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it.

org.openaq.amqp.Measurement.name: Measurement

subject.description: AMQP message subject matching the CloudEvent subject and Kafka key for the OpenAQ measurement.

parameter_name.description: OpenAQ parameter name routing attribute such as pm25, pm10, o3, no2, so2, co, or bc.

x-opt-partition-key.description: Service Bus/Event Hubs partition key matching CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

org.openaq.amqp.Measurement.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor.

org.openaq.Location.name: Location

schema.name: Location

Location.name: Location
Location.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before telemetry and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ.

location_id.description: Stable integer OpenAQ location identifier from upstream `id` or `locationsId`. Used as the first CloudEvents subject, Kafka key, MQTT topic, and AMQP partition-key segment. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

name.description: Human-readable OpenAQ location name from upstream `name`; can be null. Mutable label for display only, never a key. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

locality.description: Locality/city label from OpenAQ `locality`; can be null and is not a stable identifier. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

timezone.description: IANA timezone name for interpreting local operational context at the location, from OpenAQ `timezone`. Timestamps emitted by this feeder remain UTC/offset-aware. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

country_iso.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code supplied by OpenAQ `country.iso`; used for configuration, routing, MQTT hierarchy, and consumer filtering. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

country_name.description: Country name from the nested OpenAQ country object. Used for dashboards and attribution, not as a key. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

owner_id.description: OpenAQ owner id from nested `owner.id`; identifies the data owner/contact where provided. Null when upstream omits it. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

owner_name.description: Data owner display name from nested `owner.name`; mutable attribution label. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

provider_id.description: OpenAQ provider id from nested `provider.id`; identifies the ingestion provider/source platform. Null when absent. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

provider_name.description: OpenAQ provider display name from nested `provider.name`; useful for attribution and provider-specific quality triage. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

is_mobile.description: Boolean OpenAQ `isMobile` flag indicating whether the location represents a mobile platform rather than a fixed station. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

is_monitor.description: Boolean OpenAQ `isMonitor` flag indicating whether OpenAQ considers this location a reference monitor. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

latitude.description: WGS 84 latitude in decimal degrees from `coordinates.latitude`; null when unavailable. Unit: degrees north. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

longitude.description: WGS 84 longitude in decimal degrees from `coordinates.longitude`; null when unavailable. Unit: degrees east. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

datetime_first.description: Earliest measurement timestamp known for this location from `datetimeFirst.utc` when present. Used to understand historical coverage. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

datetime_last.description: Most recent measurement timestamp known for this location from `datetimeLast.utc` when present. Used to prioritize active feeds. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

license.description: Semicolon-separated license names/attribution strings from OpenAQ `licenses`; preserve for downstream attribution and compliance. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

sensor_count.description: Number of parameter-specific sensors listed in the location payload. Useful for completeness checks and dashboard summaries. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

schema.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before telemetry and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ.

org.openaq.Location.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before telemetry and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ.

org.openaq.Sensor.name: Sensor

schema.name: Sensor

Sensor.name: Sensor
Sensor.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and latest value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it.

location_id.description: Stable integer OpenAQ location identifier from upstream `id` or `locationsId`. Used as the first CloudEvents subject, Kafka key, MQTT topic, and AMQP partition-key segment. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

sensor_id.description: Stable integer OpenAQ sensor identifier from upstream `id` or `sensorsId`. Identifies one parameter-specific stream at a location. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

country_iso.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code supplied by OpenAQ `country.iso`; used for configuration, routing, MQTT hierarchy, and consumer filtering. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

sensor_name.description: OpenAQ sensor name from upstream `name`; typically derived from parameter and location. Mutable label, not a key. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

parameter_id.description: OpenAQ parameter identifier from the parameter object. Joins to `/v3/parameters`; stable across locations and sensors. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

parameter_name.description: OpenAQ machine-readable parameter name, for example pm25, pm10, o3, no2, so2, co, bc, temperature, relativehumidity, pressure, or windspeed. Used as a routing segment; not localized. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

lang:en.pm25: Fine particulate matter with aerodynamic diameter <= 2.5 µm; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³.
lang:en.pm10: Particulate matter with aerodynamic diameter <= 10 µm; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³.
lang:en.o3: Ozone concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³, ppb, or ppm depending on provider.
lang:en.no2: Nitrogen dioxide concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³, ppb, or ppm depending on provider.
lang:en.so2: Sulfur dioxide concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³, ppb, or ppm depending on provider.
lang:en.co: Carbon monoxide concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³, ppm, or ppb depending on provider.
lang:en.bc: Black carbon concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³.
lang:en.no: Nitric oxide concentration; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.nox: Nitrogen oxides concentration; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.pm1: Particulate matter with aerodynamic diameter <= 1 µm; commonly reported in µg/m³.
lang:en.co2: Carbon dioxide concentration; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.temperature: Ambient temperature measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.relativehumidity: Relative humidity measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.pressure: Atmospheric pressure measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.windspeed: Wind speed measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.winddirection: Wind direction measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.um003: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.03 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um005: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.05 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um010: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.10 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um025: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.25 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um050: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.50 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um100: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 1.00 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.

parameter_units.description: Unit string as published by OpenAQ `/v3/parameters` or nested parameter metadata, commonly µg/m³ for particulate and gas mass concentrations and ppm/ppb for some gas feeds. Preserve exactly for consumers that need unit-aware conversions. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

parameter_display_name.description: Human-friendly parameter label from `parameter.displayName`; may be null. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

datetime_first.description: Earliest timestamp for this sensor stream from `datetimeFirst.utc`; null when OpenAQ has no coverage metadata. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

datetime_last.description: Latest timestamp for this sensor stream from `datetimeLast.utc`; used with measurement dedupe. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

latest_value.description: Latest value embedded in the sensor reference payload when present. Unit is `parameter_units`; telemetry consumers should use Measurement events for time-series values. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

latest_datetime.description: Timestamp of `latest_value` from nested `latest.datetime.utc`; null when the sensor reference does not include latest data. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

latitude.description: WGS 84 latitude of the latest sensor value when present in nested `latest.coordinates`; unit degrees north. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

longitude.description: WGS 84 longitude of the latest sensor value when present in nested `latest.coordinates`; unit degrees east. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

schema.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and latest value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it.

org.openaq.Sensor.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and latest value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it.

org.openaq.Measurement.name: Measurement

schema.name: Measurement

Measurement.name: Measurement
Measurement.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor.

location_id.description: Stable integer OpenAQ location identifier from upstream `id` or `locationsId`. Used as the first CloudEvents subject, Kafka key, MQTT topic, and AMQP partition-key segment. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

sensor_id.description: Stable integer OpenAQ sensor identifier from upstream `id` or `sensorsId`. Identifies one parameter-specific stream at a location. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

country_iso.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code supplied by OpenAQ `country.iso`; used for configuration, routing, MQTT hierarchy, and consumer filtering. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

parameter_id.description: OpenAQ parameter identifier from the parameter object. Joins to `/v3/parameters`; stable across locations and sensors. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

parameter_name.description: OpenAQ machine-readable parameter name, for example pm25, pm10, o3, no2, so2, co, bc, temperature, relativehumidity, pressure, or windspeed. Used as a routing segment; not localized. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

lang:en.pm25: Fine particulate matter with aerodynamic diameter <= 2.5 µm; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³.
lang:en.pm10: Particulate matter with aerodynamic diameter <= 10 µm; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³.
lang:en.o3: Ozone concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³, ppb, or ppm depending on provider.
lang:en.no2: Nitrogen dioxide concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³, ppb, or ppm depending on provider.
lang:en.so2: Sulfur dioxide concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³, ppb, or ppm depending on provider.
lang:en.co: Carbon monoxide concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³, ppm, or ppb depending on provider.
lang:en.bc: Black carbon concentration; commonly reported by OpenAQ in µg/m³.
lang:en.no: Nitric oxide concentration; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.nox: Nitrogen oxides concentration; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.pm1: Particulate matter with aerodynamic diameter <= 1 µm; commonly reported in µg/m³.
lang:en.co2: Carbon dioxide concentration; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.temperature: Ambient temperature measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.relativehumidity: Relative humidity measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.pressure: Atmospheric pressure measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.windspeed: Wind speed measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.winddirection: Wind direction measurement; units are carried in parameter_units exactly as OpenAQ publishes them.
lang:en.um003: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.03 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um005: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.05 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um010: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.10 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um025: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.25 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um050: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 0.50 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.
lang:en.um100: Ultrafine particle count channel for particles >= 1.00 µm; units are carried in parameter_units.

parameter_units.description: Unit string as published by OpenAQ `/v3/parameters` or nested parameter metadata, commonly µg/m³ for particulate and gas mass concentrations and ppm/ppb for some gas feeds. Preserve exactly for consumers that need unit-aware conversions. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

datetime.description: Observation timestamp from OpenAQ `datetime.utc` for the latest measurement. This is the time the value represents, not the bridge polling time. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

value.description: Measured concentration or meteorological value from OpenAQ `value`. Unit is carried in `parameter_units`; common air pollutant units include µg/m³ for pm25, pm10, o3, no2, so2, co, and bc, with ppm/ppb appearing for some providers. Null values are preserved when upstream flags a record without a numeric value. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

latitude.description: WGS 84 latitude attached to the measurement from `coordinates.latitude`; unit degrees north. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

longitude.description: WGS 84 longitude attached to the measurement from `coordinates.longitude`; unit degrees east. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

is_valid.description: Boolean validity summary derived from OpenAQ `flagInfo.hasFlags` when the `/v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest` payload includes that optional extension: false when flags are present, true when no flags are present, null when the latest endpoint omits flag info. Definitive historical flag detail is available from OpenAQ flag/measurement endpoints outside this real-time latest-feed scope. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

has_flags.description: OpenAQ flag summary from `flagInfo.hasFlags` when the `/v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest` payload includes that optional extension; null when the latest endpoint omits flag metadata. Full flag windows are intentionally out of scope for the real-time latest poller. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

poll_time.description: UTC time when the bridge fetched and emitted this latest measurement. This supports latency monitoring and replay diagnostics; it is not an upstream observation time. Source: bridge runtime.

schema.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor.

org.openaq.Measurement.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor.

schema.name: Location
schema.doc: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before telemetry and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.

fields.name: location_id
fields.doc: Stable integer OpenAQ location identifier from upstream `id` or `locationsId`. Used as the first CloudEvents subject, Kafka key, MQTT topic, and AMQP partition-key segment. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: name
fields.doc: Human-readable OpenAQ location name from upstream `name`; can be null. Mutable label for display only, never a key. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: locality
fields.doc: Locality/city label from OpenAQ `locality`; can be null and is not a stable identifier. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: timezone
fields.doc: IANA timezone name for interpreting local operational context at the location, from OpenAQ `timezone`. Timestamps emitted by this feeder remain UTC/offset-aware. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: country_iso
fields.doc: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code supplied by OpenAQ `country.iso`; used for configuration, routing, MQTT hierarchy, and consumer filtering. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: country_name
fields.doc: Country name from the nested OpenAQ country object. Used for dashboards and attribution, not as a key. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: owner_id
fields.doc: OpenAQ owner id from nested `owner.id`; identifies the data owner/contact where provided. Null when upstream omits it. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: owner_name
fields.doc: Data owner display name from nested `owner.name`; mutable attribution label. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: provider_id
fields.doc: OpenAQ provider id from nested `provider.id`; identifies the ingestion provider/source platform. Null when absent. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: provider_name
fields.doc: OpenAQ provider display name from nested `provider.name`; useful for attribution and provider-specific quality triage. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: is_mobile
fields.doc: Boolean OpenAQ `isMobile` flag indicating whether the location represents a mobile platform rather than a fixed station. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: is_monitor
fields.doc: Boolean OpenAQ `isMonitor` flag indicating whether OpenAQ considers this location a reference monitor. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: latitude
fields.doc: WGS 84 latitude in decimal degrees from `coordinates.latitude`; null when unavailable. Unit: degrees north. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: longitude
fields.doc: WGS 84 longitude in decimal degrees from `coordinates.longitude`; null when unavailable. Unit: degrees east. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: datetime_first
fields.doc: Earliest measurement timestamp known for this location from `datetimeFirst.utc` when present. Used to understand historical coverage. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: datetime_last
fields.doc: Most recent measurement timestamp known for this location from `datetimeLast.utc` when present. Used to prioritize active feeds. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: license
fields.doc: Semicolon-separated license names/attribution strings from OpenAQ `licenses`; preserve for downstream attribution and compliance. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: sensor_count
fields.doc: Number of parameter-specific sensors listed in the location payload. Useful for completeness checks and dashboard summaries. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

schema.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before telemetry and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.

org.openaq.Location.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ monitoring location from API v3 `GET /v3/locations`. It identifies a physical or mobile air-quality monitoring site, its WGS 84 coordinates, country, ownership/provider context, monitor/mobile flags, license attribution, and first/last observation dates. Emitted before telemetry and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret measurements without separately calling OpenAQ. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.

schema.name: Sensor
schema.doc: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and latest value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.

fields.name: location_id
fields.doc: Stable integer OpenAQ location identifier from upstream `id` or `locationsId`. Used as the first CloudEvents subject, Kafka key, MQTT topic, and AMQP partition-key segment. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: sensor_id
fields.doc: Stable integer OpenAQ sensor identifier from upstream `id` or `sensorsId`. Identifies one parameter-specific stream at a location. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: country_iso
fields.doc: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code supplied by OpenAQ `country.iso`; used for configuration, routing, MQTT hierarchy, and consumer filtering. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: sensor_name
fields.doc: OpenAQ sensor name from upstream `name`; typically derived from parameter and location. Mutable label, not a key. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: parameter_id
fields.doc: OpenAQ parameter identifier from the parameter object. Joins to `/v3/parameters`; stable across locations and sensors. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: parameter_name

type.name: ParameterName
type.doc: OpenAQ machine-readable parameter name. Symbol meanings match the JsonStructure parameter_name enum descriptions; units remain dynamic in parameter_units.

fields.doc: OpenAQ machine-readable parameter name, for example pm25, pm10, o3, no2, so2, co, bc, temperature, relativehumidity, pressure, or windspeed. Used as a routing segment; not localized. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: parameter_units
fields.doc: Unit string as published by OpenAQ `/v3/parameters` or nested parameter metadata, commonly µg/m³ for particulate and gas mass concentrations and ppm/ppb for some gas feeds. Preserve exactly for consumers that need unit-aware conversions. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: parameter_display_name
fields.doc: Human-friendly parameter label from `parameter.displayName`; may be null. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: datetime_first
fields.doc: Earliest timestamp for this sensor stream from `datetimeFirst.utc`; null when OpenAQ has no coverage metadata. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: datetime_last
fields.doc: Latest timestamp for this sensor stream from `datetimeLast.utc`; used with measurement dedupe. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: latest_value
fields.doc: Latest value embedded in the sensor reference payload when present. Unit is `parameter_units`; telemetry consumers should use Measurement events for time-series values. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: latest_datetime
fields.doc: Timestamp of `latest_value` from nested `latest.datetime.utc`; null when the sensor reference does not include latest data. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: latitude
fields.doc: WGS 84 latitude of the latest sensor value when present in nested `latest.coordinates`; unit degrees north. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: longitude
fields.doc: WGS 84 longitude of the latest sensor value when present in nested `latest.coordinates`; unit degrees east. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

schema.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and latest value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.

org.openaq.Sensor.description: Reference metadata for one OpenAQ sensor from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/sensors`. A sensor represents one parameter-specific measurement stream at a location, carrying the stable sensor id, sensor name, parameter id/name/units, date coverage, and latest value metadata when OpenAQ exposes it. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.

schema.name: Measurement
schema.doc: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.

fields.name: location_id
fields.doc: Stable integer OpenAQ location identifier from upstream `id` or `locationsId`. Used as the first CloudEvents subject, Kafka key, MQTT topic, and AMQP partition-key segment. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: sensor_id
fields.doc: Stable integer OpenAQ sensor identifier from upstream `id` or `sensorsId`. Identifies one parameter-specific stream at a location. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: country_iso
fields.doc: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code supplied by OpenAQ `country.iso`; used for configuration, routing, MQTT hierarchy, and consumer filtering. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: parameter_id
fields.doc: OpenAQ parameter identifier from the parameter object. Joins to `/v3/parameters`; stable across locations and sensors. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: parameter_name

type.name: ParameterName
type.doc: OpenAQ machine-readable parameter name. Symbol meanings match the JsonStructure parameter_name enum descriptions; units remain dynamic in parameter_units.

fields.doc: OpenAQ machine-readable parameter name, for example pm25, pm10, o3, no2, so2, co, bc, temperature, relativehumidity, pressure, or windspeed. Used as a routing segment; not localized. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: parameter_units
fields.doc: Unit string as published by OpenAQ `/v3/parameters` or nested parameter metadata, commonly µg/m³ for particulate and gas mass concentrations and ppm/ppb for some gas feeds. Preserve exactly for consumers that need unit-aware conversions. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: datetime
fields.doc: Observation timestamp from OpenAQ `datetime.utc` for the latest measurement. This is the time the value represents, not the bridge polling time. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: value
fields.doc: Measured concentration or meteorological value from OpenAQ `value`. Unit is carried in `parameter_units`; common air pollutant units include µg/m³ for pm25, pm10, o3, no2, so2, co, and bc, with ppm/ppb appearing for some providers. Null values are preserved when upstream flags a record without a numeric value. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: latitude
fields.doc: WGS 84 latitude attached to the measurement from `coordinates.latitude`; unit degrees north. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: longitude
fields.doc: WGS 84 longitude attached to the measurement from `coordinates.longitude`; unit degrees east. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: is_valid
fields.doc: Boolean validity summary derived from OpenAQ `flagInfo.hasFlags` when the `/v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest` payload includes that optional extension: false when flags are present, true when no flags are present, null when the latest endpoint omits flag info. Definitive historical flag detail is available from OpenAQ flag/measurement endpoints outside this real-time latest-feed scope. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: has_flags
fields.doc: OpenAQ flag summary from `flagInfo.hasFlags` when the `/v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest` payload includes that optional extension; null when the latest endpoint omits flag metadata. Full flag windows are intentionally out of scope for the real-time latest poller. Source: OpenAQ API v3 docs (https://api.openaq.org/openapi.json, https://docs.openaq.org/).

fields.name: poll_time
fields.doc: UTC time when the bridge fetched and emitted this latest measurement. This supports latency monitoring and replay diagnostics; it is not an upstream observation time. Source: bridge runtime.

schema.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.

org.openaq.Measurement.description: Latest air-quality measurement for one OpenAQ sensor/location pair from API v3 `GET /v3/locations/{locations_id}/latest`. It carries the observed concentration or meteorological value, parameter metadata and units, timestamp, and coordinates. The bridge polls latest values and emits only changed timestamps/values per sensor. Avro mirror of the JsonStructure schema; keep field semantics and nullability in sync.
